metadata.title: Research BRCA x PARP Synthetic Lethality: Why Does It Work in Some Cancers and Not Others?

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: The 2x2 grid made it clear that only BRCA-mutant-plus-PARP-inhibited is lethal, because it eliminates both repair paths, and that reversion mutations rescue the tumor. I want to understand why the same geometry fails in most solid tumors. Can you (1) explain what 'BRCAness' means and why some HR-deficient tumors without BRCA mutations still respond, (2) describe how a reversion mutation restores HR repair mechanistically, and (3) compare why MTAP-PRMT5 sits earlier in the pipeline than BRCA-PARP?

props.command: The 2x2 grid made it clear that only BRCA-mutant-plus-PARP-inhibited is lethal, because it eliminates both repair paths, and that reversion mutations rescue the tumor. I want to understand why the same geometry fails in most solid tumors. Can you (1) explain what 'BRCAness' means and why some HR-deficient tumors without BRCA mutations still respond, (2) describe how a reversion mutation restores HR repair mechanistically, and (3) compare why MTAP-PRMT5 sits earlier in the pipeline than BRCA-PARP?
props.segment: Research BRCA x PARP Synthetic Lethality:

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Synthetic lethality — context is everything.

PARP inhibitors produce deep remissions in BRCA-mutant ovarian cancer. The same molecular logic fails in most solid tumors. Why does the same geometric relationship between two vulnerabilities work in some contexts and not others? The answer is not in the drug. It is in whether the tumor's specific defect actually aligns with a druggable partner.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- title card: BRCA/PARP synthetic lethality; context-dependent failure highlighted

beats.narration_text: In the terminal: ask Claude to research the BRCA/PARP paradigm — clinical evidence, resistance mechanisms, and extensions.

props.command:
claude "Research synthetic lethality: BRCA/PARP paradigm.
Find clinical evidence for success (olaparib, rucaparib,
niraparib approvals), the most common resistance
mechanisms, and the three most promising extensions
beyond BRCA. For each extension, cite clinical trial
status and objective response rates."

beats.role_note: terminal (Onda) -- Python script reading Claude response
beats.narration_text: Read the code before trusting it. The script builds the synthetic lethality matrix from Claude's response — rows are clinical context, columns are whether it works, the main resistance mechanism, and the objective response rate. Read the matrix before accepting any claim.

props.code:
# synthetic_lethality_matrix.py -- Claude Code output
MATRIX = [
  {"context": "BRCA1/2-mutant ovarian",
   "works": "YES", "orr": "54-72%",
   "resistance": "BRCA reversion mutation"},
  {"context": "BRCA-mutant breast",
   "works": "YES", "orr": "59%",
   "resistance": "BRCA reversion mutation"},
  {"context": "Most solid tumors",
   "works": "NO", "orr": "<10%",
   "resistance": "Geometry mismatch"},
]
print(f"{'Context':<28} {'Works':<6} {'ORR':<8} Resistance")
print("-" * 65)
for r in MATRIX:
  print(f"{r['context']:<28} {r['works']:<6} {r['orr']:<8} {r['resistance']}")

beats.narration_text: Run it. The 2x2 grid reveals: BRCA wild-type with PARP active — the cell survives. BRCA wild-type with PARP inhibited — also survives, HR repair compensates. BRCA mutant with PARP active — survives, because there is still one working repair path. BRCA mutant with PARP inhibited — lethal. Both repair paths eliminated. Below: reversion mutation is the resistance mechanism.
beats.visual_intent: Manim 2x2 grid: BRCA status x PARP status; LETHAL cell in CRIMSON; resistance note below

beats.narration_text: Now push the question: does the same logic work for other synthetic lethal pairs? Add the MTAP-PRMT5 pair and compare the evidence strength.

props.command:
claude "Add the MTAP-PRMT5 synthetic lethal pair.
Compare the evidence strength for MTAP-PRMT5
versus original BRCA/PARP -- cite the most
advanced clinical trial for each."

beats.narration_text: The extended grid. BRCA/PARP confirmed and FDA-approved. MTAP-PRMT5 appears below with a Phase 2 badge and a weaker fill — same geometric logic, earlier in the pipeline. The evidence strength contrast is visible: confirmed versus emerging.
beats.visual_intent: Manim: BRCA/PARP confirmed row + MTAP-PRMT5 Phase 2 row with CRIMSON/GOLD fill for emerging status

beats.narration_text: Synthetic lethality is a geometric relationship between two vulnerabilities. It works when the tumor's specific defect aligns with a druggable partner. Translation fails when the geometry doesn't hold — when the second defect is not druggable, or when the tumor has alternative repair pathways the drug cannot reach.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- summary card: geometric relationship defined; context-dependence highlighted

beats.narration_text: Before prescribing any PARP inhibitor, verify the specific BRCA mutation and whether it causes genuine HR deficiency. Reversion mutations mean the geometric relationship has been repaired by the tumor. Germline versus somatic BRCA status changes the eligibility criteria. Test first.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- next steps card: verify HR deficiency, check reversion status, germline vs somatic

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Here's what the body just demonstrated. Synthetic lethality is a geometric relationship between two vulnerabilities. It works when the tumor's specific defect aligns with a druggable partner. Translation fails when the geometry doesn't hold — when the second defect is not druggable, or when the tumor has alternative repair pathways the drug cannot reach. Before prescribing any PARP inhibitor, verify the specific BRCA mutation and whether it causes genuine HR deficiency. Reversion mutations mean the geometric relationship has been repaired by the tumor. Germline versus somatic BRCA status changes the eligibility criteria. Test first.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Research BRCA x PARP Synthetic Lethality: Why Does It Work in Some Can] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
